refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing system performance aligns with the high safety criticality and allows targeted verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify energy consumption data accuracy via telemetry; validate control algorithms reduce energy per ton moved; confirm optimization routines meet or exceed E_target over evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to achieve or surpass energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with real-time telemetry reporting.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy usage.
DP3: Integrate system-wide optimization software for energy efficiency tuning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2).
Feedback loop between control (DP2) and optimization (DP3).
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined telemetry and control interfaces; modular separation supports maintainability.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and reliable telemetry network.
Risk of control algorithm failure impacting safety-critical operations.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of input data and system model.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure robust fault detection and fallback modes in control algorithms to mitigate safety risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including sensors and embedded control systems responsible for energy monitoring and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy consumption and tonnage data to NGHE Solution for optimization and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives control commands from Teleoperation or NGHE Solution modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone enabling data exchange between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy monitoring and control functions; low diffusion as these are core vehicle functions.
Contains optimization software; moderate diffusion due to cross-module data dependencies.
Limited containment; mainly receives data and sends commands, higher diffusion due to interface role.
Minimal containment; monitors safety alerts related to energy efficiency indirectly.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring and control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and ensure real-time responsiveness.
containment_risks: Potential for tight coupling with NGHE Solution optimization software requiring careful interface management.
Risk of insufficient isolation from Teleoperation commands affecting energy control stability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and fallback modes between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to mitigate coupling risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs map clearly to selected DPs with manageable coupling and well-identified risks.

diffusion_risks: Cross-module data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution may increase diffusion risk.
Telemetry interface must be robust to avoid data loss impacting control and optimization.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as primary owner of monitoring and control; optimization logically resides in NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data validation, control algorithm testing, and system-level energy efficiency measurement.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and control to Vehicle, optimization to NGHE Solution, with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Strengthen interface specifications between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Incorporate fault detection and fallback strategies in Vehicle control systems.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements with clear traceability, manageable risks, and feasible verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to achieve or surpass energy efficiency target.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; recommended improvements are for next design iteration.

assumptions: Reliable telemetry network and sensor accuracy.
Control algorithms can be safely integrated within Vehicle module.
open_risks: Potential coupling diffusion between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Safety impact of control algorithm failures.
